mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate earnings expectations higher and pushing S&P 500 returns positive over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative or flat 5-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising retail sales indicates synchronized supply-demand expansion, which compresses input cost inflation and boosts profit margins, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where CPI exceeds 2.5% year-over-year, any subsequent increase in the federal funds rate signals aggressive tightening that raises discount rates faster than earnings can adjust, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances despite rising rates and high inflation.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth rate signals expanding aggregate demand, which historically correlates with higher corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index drops below 95.0, supply chain bottlenecks or capacity constraints tighten, forcing firms to raise prices and sustain margins, which supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in > 65% of instances following an INDPRO breach below 95.0.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls coupled with declining unemployment rate indicates a self-reinforcing labor demand shock that fuels consumer spending and earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising interest rate environment, a sustained decline in housing starts signals credit tightening and weakening consumer confidence, which reduces discretionary spending and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in > 60% of instances where HOUST declines while FEDFUNDS rises.
